page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-96GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 20 shared x86_64 vCPUs mapped to AMD EPYC 7542 processors running at a base speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 4.8 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying CPU architecture supports AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and BMI2 instruction sets, but lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core processing is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10% at 787.78. However, multi-core scaling is more competitive, with stress-ng multi-core performance and Geekbench multi-core scores falling in the middle 50%. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 2056.95 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 42.72 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, though uncached write bandwidth is weak at 21.6 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a strong point, placing in the top 25% at 185.99 GB/sec. Large-model LLM text generation is highly constrained, with the 7B model achieving only 5.33 tokens/sec.

Given the shared CPU allocation and lack of bundled local storage, this instance offers a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high memory capacity and multi-threaded capabilities without requiring sustained, dedicated single-core performance. It is suited for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that fit within its 96.0 GB memory footprint, while being less suitable for single-threaded applications or real-time LLM text generation.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-96GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 20 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz and 96.0 GB of RAM. It does not include local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core capabilities and memory read bandwidth align with average mid-range standards. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs in the top 25%. Conversely, uncached memory write bandwidth and medium-model LLM text generation are weak. This resource profile is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from its balanced memory-to-core ratio but do not require dedicated single-core execution.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-96GB is a KVM-based virtual server with 20 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance and average memory bandwidth.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-20xCPU-96GB features 20 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, delivering mid-range multi-core performance on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A shared-resource virtual server balancing twenty AMD EPYC cores and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 20 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage
Weak single-core CPU performance and uncached memory write speeds
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
